pursuitEconomics.content:
RingCentral (NYSE: RNG) is a compelling pursuit at a strategic inflection point — a $2.5B revenue company pivoting hard into agentic voice AI. FY2025 total revenue reached $2.52B (up 4.8% YoY), subscription revenue hit $2.43B (up 5.6%), and free cash flow surged 32% to over $530M EXT. This is a profitable, cash-generating business with real AI momentum, not vaporware.

The AI economics are striking: AI-utilizing customers now represent nearly 10% of total ARR, with AI products tripling year-over-year to reach a $100M ARR exit rate EXT. AI Receptionist (AIR) alone reached 8,300 customers in Q4 2025, up 44% sequentially from 5,800 in Q3 EXT. The company explicitly states that AI-utilizing customers drive higher usage, increased spend, and stronger retention EXT. This Group PM role sits at the center of this growth engine — owning agentic voice AI product lines that represent RingCentral's primary strategic vector.

Compensation analysis: The posted range of $142,800–$204,000 base JD is the floor for a GPM-level role. Levels.fyi reports RingCentral PM total comp at $222K–$250K+, with GPMs averaging $175K base + $150K stock equity EXT. Target total comp of $300K–$400K including RSUs and bonus. RNG initiated a quarterly $0.075/share dividend and expanded its share buyback to $500M EXT, signaling equity value stability. 2026 guidance projects 4.5%–5.5% subscription revenue growth with $580M–$600M free cash flow and non-GAAP EPS of $4.76–$4.97 (up 11% YoY at midpoint) EXT.

Equity upside: RNG stock has recovered 65% over the past 12 months EXT. AI products growing at 3x while the core business grows ~5% creates a two-speed company. If the GPM can drive AIR and related products from $100M to $250M+ ARR, that directly impacts valuation multiples. The company is also insulated from the February 2026 tariff disruption due to its software-first model — RNG gained 4% the day tariffs were announced EXT.

existence.content:
This role demonstrably exists and is in active hiring. The position is posted on RingCentral's Workday careers portal as Group Product Manager (R075595) EXT, confirming approved headcount. The GPM title at RingCentral implies ownership of multiple product managers and a full product line — a senior IC/player-coach hybrid with meaningful scope.

The role sits within the AI product family, which is RingCentral's undisputed growth engine. The company's AI product ecosystem spans three products across the full conversation lifecycle: AIR (AI Receptionist) for inbound call handling with 8,300+ customers; AVA (AI Virtual Assistant) for real-time mid-conversation agent assist; and ACE (AI Conversation Expert) for post-call analysis and coaching, with 4,800+ customers EXT. Most recently, RingCentral unveiled AIR Pro at Enterprise Connect 2026 (March 10, 2026) — a voice-first agentic AI platform that lets businesses build and deploy autonomous AI agents across voice, SMS, chat, and digital channels using a no-code studio EXT.

The JD emphasizes defining intelligent agent behaviors grounded in NLU/NLP, LLMs, memory, reasoning, and dialog orchestration JD, indicating this is a core AI product role building the reasoning layer, not a wrapper on someone else's API. The GPM would own the product strategy for these agent behaviors.

Organizational context: Kira Makagon was promoted to President and COO in February 2025, overseeing product, technology, GTM, and operations EXT. Srinivasan (Srini) Raghavan served as Chief Product Officer, having joined from Five9 (previously Cisco Collaboration), but departed to become CPO at Freshworks in late 2024 EXT. The current CPO succession is unclear — Antonio Nucci, former CTO at Aisera and Cisco, joined as Chief AI Officer in May 2025 EXT. This GPM role likely reports to a VP or Sr. Director of Product under the AI org, with dotted-line influence from Nucci's AI team. The CPO vacancy could mean either Makagon is directly overseeing product, or a replacement is incoming — a good question for the interview.

companyMarket.content:
Company Position

RingCentral is a 27-year-old cloud communications incumbent with $2.5B+ revenue, now aggressively pivoting into AI-powered communications. Gartner's 2025 Magic Quadrant names RingCentral as a Leader in UCaaS alongside Cisco, Microsoft, and Zoom EXT. The company operates three core product pillars: UCaaS (RingEX unified communications), CCaaS (RingCX contact center), and CPaaS (communications APIs) EXT.

The strategic shift: RingCentral has evolved from a pure UCaaS play to an AI-first communications platform. In February 2026, RingCentral announced a deep integration with OpenAI, embedding GPT-5.2 into live voice calls across AIR, AVA, and ACE products — running OpenAI's reasoning capabilities on RingCentral's low-latency voice infrastructure EXT. This is not a surface-level chatbot integration; it brings generative AI into the real-time flow of enterprise voice conversations.

Market Size & Growth

The global UCaaS market reached $33.4B in 2024, growing at a 13.2% CAGR through 2036 to a projected $37.2B in 2026 EXT. CCaaS is growing faster at 15–25% annually, driven by PBX-to-cloud migrations EXT. RingCentral targets a $93B+ TAM spanning UCaaS, CCaaS, and agentic AI EXT. The convergence of UCaaS + CCaaS + AI ("MultiCaaS") is the defining trend — and RingCentral's integrated telephony infrastructure gives it a moat that pure-AI startups cannot replicate.

Key Recent Developments (Feb–Mar 2026)

  • OpenAI partnership announced Feb 19, 2026 — GPT-5.2 embedded across all AI products EXT
  • AIR Pro launched at Enterprise Connect Mar 10, 2026 — voice-first agentic AI platform with no-code studio, healthcare vertical templates, multilingual mid-conversation switching EXT
  • Spectrum Business partnership expanded Mar 11, 2026 — RingCX and ACE available nationwide to Spectrum Business customers via UCX EXT
  • IDC MarketScape named RingCentral a Leader in AI-Enabled Contact Center Workforce Engagement Management EXT
  • RingWEM (Workforce Engagement Management) suite launched Nov 2025, integrating AI-powered forecasting from CommunityWFM acquisition EXT

Competitive Landscape

CompetitorStrengthWeakness vs. RingCentral
Microsoft TeamsBundled distribution via M365, enterprise lock-inWeaker telephony/PSTN; RingCentral launched "RingCentral for Microsoft Teams 2.0" positioning compatibility as advantage
ZoomUX, brand recognition, growing CCaaSSmaller telephony installed base, less enterprise penetration
Genesys / Five9CCaaS-native, deep CX featuresNo UCaaS platform, integration complexity for unified comms
DialpadAI-first approach, mid-market strengthSmaller scale, narrower product suite
Retell AI / Eleven LabsAI-native voice quality, cutting-edge synthesisNo telephony infrastructure, no enterprise distribution or compliance

Enterprise customers now represent over 50% of RingCentral's ARR, reflecting a strategic shift from SMB-focused to enterprise EXT. The pricing model has evolved from per-seat to value-based tiers incorporating premium AI features — a transition the GPM would directly influence.

plausible.content:
Ili's candidacy is strong — arguably top-decile for this specific role, because the intersection of telecom + AI + senior product leadership is genuinely rare in the candidate pool.

The JD's hardest requirement is strong understanding of SIP/PSTN basics, call routing, IVR/receptionist flows JD — Ili's telecom career background directly addresses this, a skillset most product managers simply don't have ILI. In a world where most AI PM candidates come from software/web backgrounds, someone who understands telephony infrastructure at a deep level is a significant hiring advantage for RingCentral. This is the primary differentiator.

The AI product focus maps directly: the role requires defining intelligent agent behaviors grounded in NLU/NLP, LLMs, memory, reasoning, and dialog orchestration JD. Ili's stated focus on AI product leadership ILI and 15+ years of senior product experience across data products, platform strategy, and programmatic ecosystems ILI positions well for the intersection of telecom infrastructure + AI agents + enterprise platform.

Level calibration: GPM is typically one rung below Sr. Director/VP. Given Ili's seniority, this is worth understanding — but at RingCentral, the GPM role for AI products has outsized scope. The AI product family is in hyper-growth at $100M ARR with 3x YoY trajectory EXT, and the company just launched AIR Pro — an entirely new agentic AI platform — at Enterprise Connect 2026 EXT. The GPM role here likely has more product impact than a Director title at a slower-growth company.

screenOut.content:
No hard screen-outs identified. Potential yellow flags to address proactively:

  • Title calibration: GPM is typically one level below Sr. Director/VP. With 15+ years of experience, confirm the scope justifies the level. Mitigant: The AI product line's $100M→$250M+ growth trajectory gives this role outsized impact. Also note RingCentral has a separate Sr. Director of Product posting EXT — if the GPM feels narrow, the Sr. Dir role could be an alternative.
  • Location: RingCentral's HQ is in Belmont, CA, with significant presence in Denver and internationally EXT. Confirm remote/hybrid policy for this specific role — RingCentral's stance has varied by team.
  • Ad tech → UCaaS transition: Ili's primary domain is ad tech and data products ILI. The telecom background bridges this gap, but expect interview questions about UCaaS/CCaaS-specific experience. Prepare concrete examples of platform extensibility, API strategy, and enterprise go-to-market from prior roles.
  • CPO vacancy risk: Former CPO Srini Raghavan departed to Freshworks in late 2024 EXT. No public replacement announced. This could mean product org instability — or opportunity (new CPO may want to hire their own team). Ask about reporting structure and product org leadership plans.

Culture intelligence: RingCentral's Glassdoor rating is 3.5/5 with 1,975 reviews; 57% recommending, 45% positive outlook EXT. Key concerns include organizational silos, frequent restructuring (layoffs reported quarterly), and management described as "an engineering company led by accountants" EXT. However, the AI team is the growth engine receiving heavy investment — it may operate with more autonomy than the core UCaaS org. Probe this directly in interviews.

needs.content:
Reading between the JD lines and triangulating with recent company developments, RingCentral needs someone who can:

  1. Scale agentic AI from $100M to $250M+ ARR: AI products tripled YoY but represent only 10% of total revenue EXT. The GPM must drive the next 2–3x through product-market fit refinement, pricing strategy, vertical expansion, and enterprise adoption playbooks. AIR Pro just launched with healthcare as the first vertical, with financial services, retail, and professional services planned next EXT.
  2. Build the platform layer for agentic AI: The JD explicitly calls for building scalable, reusable frameworks for agent development, configuration, and lifecycle management across diverse enterprise and consumer use cases JD. AIR Pro Studio (no-code agent builder) is the early expression of this — but the underlying platform architecture needs a PM who thinks in primitives, not features.
  3. Bridge telephony infrastructure and frontier AI: SIP/PSTN, call routing, IVR expertise is explicitly required JD because the AI layer must integrate deeply with existing telephony infrastructure. The OpenAI partnership embeds GPT-5.2 into live voice calls on RingCentral's low-latency infrastructure EXT — the GPM needs to understand both sides of this stack.
  4. Drive enterprise adoption of AIR: AIR sees particularly good uptake among smaller customers due to ease of setup, per COO Kira Makagon EXT. The challenge: scaling to enterprise with customization, compliance (healthcare, financial services), and deep system integration. A healthcare customer expanded AIR usage from 30,000 to 500,000 minutes quarterly after a successful pilot — reducing call handling by 50% and escalations by 40% EXT. The GPM needs to replicate this pattern across verticals.
  5. Define measurement and experimentation: Partner with design and research teams to validate hypotheses through A/B testing JD. CFO Vaibhav Agarwal stated the company will shift to reporting AI adoption metrics periodically rather than relying on traditional cohort metrics EXT — the GPM must define what those metrics are.

fit.content:
Strong fit areas:

  • Telecom infrastructure (rare differentiator): Ili's telecom background ILI directly addresses the JD's hardest-to-fill requirement. Most AI PMs come from web/mobile; someone who understands SIP, PSTN, call routing, and IVR at a technical level is genuinely rare. This is the #1 competitive advantage in the candidate pool.
  • Platform thinking at scale: Ili's experience scaling data products and programmatic platforms ILI maps directly to the JD's emphasis on reusable frameworks, multi-vertical extensibility, and building scalable agent lifecycle management JD. The ad tech world's concept of programmatic decisioning (real-time bidding, audience targeting) parallels voice AI's real-time intent classification and routing.
  • AI product conviction: Ili's current AI product leadership focus ILI aligns with RingCentral's top strategic priority. Not a tourist exploring AI — a practitioner.
  • Data-driven product culture: The JD emphasizes A/B testing, experimentation, and measuring conversation quality JD. Ad tech backgrounds produce PMs who are naturally metrics-oriented and comfortable with experimentation frameworks.

Areas requiring interview prep:

  • UCaaS/CCaaS domain depth: RingCentral targets a $93B+ TAM spanning UCaaS, CCaaS, and agentic AI EXT. Study the product portfolio: RingEX (UCaaS), RingCX (CCaaS), AIR/AVA/ACE (AI suite), AIR Pro (agentic platform). Understand pricing tiers and competitive positioning vs. Teams/Zoom/Genesys.
  • Voice AI technical specifics: The OpenAI integration means the GPM needs fluency in the technical stack: Google ASR, OpenAI GPT-5.2 for reasoning, and low-latency voice infrastructure EXT. Prepare to discuss latency tradeoffs (sub-500ms response for natural conversation), voice quality, and conversation state management.
  • Enterprise sales motion: Enterprise customers now represent over 50% of RingCentral's ARR EXT. If prior experience skews SMB/self-serve, prepare examples of working with enterprise sales teams, navigating procurement, and managing strategic account feature requests.

gaps.content:
Gap 1: No direct UCaaS/CCaaS product experience. Ili's domain is ad tech and data products — not cloud communications. Mitigation: Telecom background provides deep infrastructure understanding; emphasize that platform thinking and AI product skills transfer directly. The JD values AI/LLM depth over UCaaS history. RingCentral is hiring for AI-first thinking, not industry tenure — the former CPO Srini Raghavan came from Five9 (CCaaS), not UCaaS EXT, showing the company values adjacent-domain hires.

Gap 2: Enterprise sales cycle experience. RingCentral sells to SMB through enterprise via direct and channel partners including AT&T, Spectrum Business, and TELUS EXT. The Spectrum partnership just expanded in March 2026 to include RingCX and ACE EXT. If Ili's PM experience was primarily self-serve or mid-market, prepare examples of working with enterprise sales teams, managing channel partner requirements, and navigating procurement cycles.

Gap 3: GPM title vs. seniority. With 15+ years, Ili may appear overqualified for GPM at face value. Mitigation: At RingCentral, this GPM owns the fastest-growing product line ($100M ARR, 3x trajectory). The scope is VP-equivalent at many companies. Also note that there's a separate Sr. Director of Product role also posted EXT — the recruiter conversation should explore both options.

Gap 4: Public company product cadence. RingCentral provides quarterly earnings guidance to Wall Street EXT and product decisions may face investor scrutiny. If Ili's experience is primarily at startups or private companies, prepare for the pace constraints and metric accountability of public-company PM work.

network.content:
Network strategy should focus on three channels:

  1. Internal contact leverage (highest priority): If the existing RingCentral Sr. Director connection (from the parallel Sr. Director of Product pursuit) can provide intel on the AI product org, team dynamics, and the hiring manager for this GPM role, activate immediately. Even if pursuing a different role, the organizational intelligence transfers. Ask specifically about: (a) who replaced Srini Raghavan as CPO (or if Kira Makagon is directly overseeing product), (b) how the AI team is structured under Antonio Nucci, and (c) the GPM hiring manager's name and style.
  2. LinkedIn research targets: Search for current RingCentral AI product team members, particularly PMs in the AI org under Antonio Nucci (Chief AI Officer, joined May 2025 from Aisera/Cisco) EXT. Also target Kira Makagon's network (President/COO, long-tenured at RingCentral) EXT for product-side connections. Look for common connections via telecom or ad tech alumni networks.
  3. External intelligence: RingCentral has 1,975 Glassdoor reviews EXT. Filter for product team reviews from the last 12 months, focusing specifically on the AI team. Also check Blind for anonymous feedback on comp bands, AI team autonomy, and whether the frequent restructuring affects the AI org. Key cultural signal: RingCentral's 2026 blog predicts organizations will shift from app-based to agent-based operations, with 97% of surveyed orgs piloting AI tools EXT — use this language in conversations to show alignment with their narrative.

interview.content:
Prepare for these likely interview topics based on the JD, recent product launches, and company context:

  1. Agentic AI product vision (most likely): "How would you evolve AIR from an inbound call handler to a fully autonomous multi-turn conversational agent?" With AIR Pro just launched at Enterprise Connect EXT, they'll want to hear your vision for the platform's next 12 months. Prepare a framework for: conversation state management, fallback handling, human-in-the-loop escalation, and measuring agent quality at scale.
  2. Platform extensibility: The JD emphasizes building reusable frameworks for agent lifecycle management across diverse use cases JD. AIR Pro Studio is the no-code expression of this. Prepare an example of building a platform product that serves multiple verticals without code forking — ad tech's programmatic infrastructure is an excellent analogy here.
  3. Telephony + AI integration (your differentiator): SIP/PSTN, call routing, IVR knowledge is required JD. Be ready to discuss: latency budgets for real-time voice AI (sub-500ms for natural conversation), ASR accuracy tradeoffs (Google ASR vs. alternatives), how GPT-5.2 reasoning runs on RingCentral's low-latency voice infrastructure EXT, and edge cases (background noise, accents, multi-party calls, DTMF tone handling).
  4. Metrics and experimentation: A/B testing, continuous optimization of conversation quality, agent accuracy, and user satisfaction JD. Prepare examples of running experiments on ML-powered products. Specific metrics to discuss: call resolution rate, containment rate, CSAT/NPS delta, average handling time reduction, escalation rate.
  5. Enterprise vs. SMB product strategy: AIR sees good adoption among smaller customers due to ease of setup (per Kira Makagon) EXT, but the growth vector is enterprise. Discuss: the healthcare customer that scaled from 30K to 500K minutes quarterly EXT as a model — what product capabilities enabled that expansion? How would you replicate it across financial services and retail?
  6. Competitive positioning: "What's our moat against Retell AI and Eleven Labs?" Answer: integrated telephony infrastructure + enterprise distribution + compliance. Pure AI startups lack PSTN connectivity and enterprise trust. Prepare a nuanced view on Microsoft Teams Copilot Voice as the longer-term competitive threat.

proofPoints.content:
Prepare these specific proof points mapped to JD requirements and recent company context:

JD RequirementWhat They're Really AskingProof Point to Prepare
Define intelligent agent behaviors with NLU/NLP, LLMs JDCan you ship AI products that actually work?Example of shipping an AI/ML-powered product feature with measurable user outcome improvement. Include: what model/approach you chose, how you evaluated quality, how you handled edge cases.
Build scalable, reusable frameworks JDCan you think in platforms, not features?Example of building a platform/infrastructure product that served multiple use cases or verticals without forking. Ad tech's programmatic stack is the perfect analogy.
SIP/PSTN, call routing, IVR expertise JDDo you speak telephony?Specific telecom project or product involving voice infrastructure. Even high-level architecture understanding of SIP trunking, PBX replacement, or IVR design counts heavily here — most candidates will have zero.
A/B testing and experimentation JDAre you data-driven or opinion-driven?Example of running experiments that changed product direction, with specific metrics and sample sizes. Bonus: ML-specific experimentation (model A/B testing, online evaluation).
Balance power-user flexibility with no-code usability JDCan you design for both technical and business users?Example of a product with both API/technical interface and self-serve/visual interface. This maps directly to AIR Pro Studio's no-code + developer API duality.
Vertical expansion (healthcare, financial services) EXTCan you take a horizontal platform into verticals?Example of adapting a horizontal product for a specific industry — compliance requirements, domain-specific workflows, specialized UX.

Format each as: "At [Company], I [specific action] which resulted in [measurable outcome with numbers]." Aim for 2-minute stories with specific numbers. Have a 30-second and 5-minute version of each.

dealBreaker.content:
Surface these in the first substantive conversation — several are especially timely given recent developments:

  1. Compensation and total comp structure: Posted range of $142,800–$204,000 base JD. Levels.fyi shows GPM total comp ~$325K (base + stock + bonus) EXT. Request explicit breakdown: base + RSU grant schedule + annual refresh + bonus target. RNG stock recovered 65% in the past year EXT — RSU grants at current prices could have meaningful upside if AI-driven revenue reaccelerates growth.
  2. Organizational stability and CPO succession: Former CPO Srini Raghavan departed to Freshworks in late 2024 EXT. Glassdoor reviews cite quarterly layoffs and frequent restructuring EXT. Ask directly: Who leads the product org now? Is Kira Makagon directly overseeing product? Is a CPO replacement planned? Has the AI team been affected by any restructuring?
  3. GPM scope and reporting: Does this GPM manage people (other PMs), or is it senior IC with group-level scope? Who is the direct hiring manager? What's the reporting chain to Chief AI Officer Antonio Nucci EXT vs. the product org? What budget and headcount authority does this role have?
  4. Location and hybrid policy: Confirm expectations — HQ is Belmont, CA with offices in Denver EXT. Some teams require 3-4 days onsite while others are fully remote. Get the specific policy for the AI product team.
  5. Product autonomy vs. corporate constraints: Reviews describe organizational silos and top-down management EXT. The AI team should have more autonomy given its growth priority — but verify. How much roadmap autonomy does the GPM have? What approval cycles exist for shipping? How are engineering resources allocated between AI and core platform?
  6. AI investment commitment: RingCentral plans ~$250M in R&D investment for 2026, heavily focused on agentic voice AI EXT. Is this budget protected if overall revenue growth slows? What happens to the AI team if the macro environment deteriorates?

nextSteps.content:
1. Leverage existing RingCentral connection (immediate): If the Sr. Director of Product contact can provide intel on the AI GPM role, hiring manager identity, or introduce you, activate today. Even org chart intel (who reports to whom in the AI team) is valuable interview prep.
  2. Study the product portfolio deeply: Before the interview, understand RingCentral's full product ecosystem:
    • RingEX — UCaaS platform (messaging, video, phone)
    • RingCX — AI-first omnichannel contact center (1,500+ customers)
    • AIR — AI Receptionist for inbound call handling (8,300+ customers)
    • AIR Pro — NEW: voice-first agentic AI platform with no-code studio (launched Mar 10, 2026)
    • AVA — AI Virtual Assistant for real-time mid-conversation agent assist
    • ACE — AI Conversation Expert for post-call analysis (4,800+ customers)
    • RingWEM — AI workforce engagement management (launched Nov 2025)
  3. Research the OpenAI partnership: RingCentral is embedding GPT-5.2 into live voice calls EXT. Understand the technical implications: latency budgets for real-time reasoning, how conversation context is maintained, privacy controls (customer data not used to train public models). Have an opinion on where this goes next.
  4. Prepare your telecom narrative: This is your single biggest differentiator. Craft a 2-minute story connecting your telecom background → platform product thinking → AI product leadership → why RingCentral's agentic voice AI is the perfect convergence of your experience. Practice it.
  5. Know the numbers: Go in knowing: $2.52B revenue, 5% growth, $530M FCF, 8,300 AIR customers (44% sequential growth), $100M AI ARR exit rate, 4.5–5.5% 2026 subscription guidance. Reference these naturally in conversation to demonstrate you've done your homework.
  6. Prepare questions that demonstrate strategic thinking: "How are you thinking about the transition from AIR (receptionist) to AIR Pro (agentic platform) — is the vision to migrate all AIR customers, or are these distinct products for distinct segments?" This shows you understand both the product portfolio and the strategic tension.
